title: Parrya runcinata
taxonomy: Parrya runcinata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a member of the genus Parrya. The plant is native to Central Asia. Within the Brassicaceae family, Parrya runcinata is closely related to other plants such as Arabis and Draba. As a result, it is often placed in the tribe Arabideae.

intro: Parrya runcinata, also known as the 'Russian thistle' or 'prickly saltwort', is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Parrya runcinata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Parrya runcinata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of herbal extracts and supplements.
